title: AICE 6xx Incident Envelope
description: Unofficial draft schema (AICE v0.4) for a single AI Chaos Engineering incident. An AICE incident records a discrepancy between narrative_state (what an agent, verifier, orchestrator, or report claims occurred) and physical_state (what is supported by observable events, independently readable artifacts, process receipts, state inspection, or verified postconditions). Canonical codes are AICE-604 through AICE-612. Unknown codes are rejected. The default workflow effect of an unresolved incident is STATE_UNCHANGED, which every incident MUST carry.

code.description: Canonical AICE identifier. The v0.4 code set is closed to AICE-604..AICE-612; any other value is rejected.

title.description: Human-readable title of the incident code. Cross-consistency with the registry title is checked by check_aice.py, not by this schema.

spec_version.description: AICE specification version this envelope conforms to.

timestamp.description: Optional ISO 8601 date-time at which the incident was emitted. Omit rather than fabricate.

claim.description: The narrative claim under scrutiny (what was reported as done).

narrative_state.description: What the producing narrative asserts about completion.

physical_state.description: What observation of the required postcondition actually supports.

observed_evidence.description: Evidence that was actually observed. May be empty.

missing_evidence.description: Required evidence that is absent or contradictory. For an emitted incident this is normally non-empty.

workflow_effect.description: Effects an unresolved incident has on workflow state. MUST contain STATE_UNCHANGED; higher-level blocking effects are additive.

required_action.description: The remediation required to resolve the incident. Additional model agreement alone is never a valid required_action.

retryability.description: Whether and how the underlying action could be resolved. Repetition of the same narrative without new evidence is never sufficient.

code_details.description: Optional escape hatch for code-specific fields not modeled by the base envelope. Deliberately permissive so codes can carry extra structure without base-schema churn.

type.description: Kind of evidence, e.g. write_event, path, stat_readback, recomputed_digest, exit_status, ci_receipt, implementation_diff, deployment_receipt, independent_evidence_path.

ref.description: Optional locator for the evidence (path, URL, run id). Never a fabricated value.

artifact_declaration.description: A declared artifact whose materialization is in question (AICE-604). The digest is represented as a declaration, not a value, so an unmaterialized artifact is never forced to carry a fabricated hash.

path.description: Confirmed path, when one exists. Null or omitted when no path was confirmed.

declared.description: True when a digest was asserted by the narrative.

value.description: The digest value, only when it was actually recomputed from observed bytes. Absent for an unmaterialized artifact.

verified_against_bytes.description: True only when the value was recomputed from independently read bytes.

value.description: A digest value may be recorded only when it was verified against observed bytes. Presence of value therefore requires verified_against_bytes: true, so no unverified (potentially fabricated) hash can be carried as fact.

actor_identity.description: Identity of a producing or verifying actor, when known.

independence_assessment.description: Independence of a verifier from the producer, evidence path, and narrative source (AICE-608).
